title: Broken JSX
description:
Welcome to Rank Challenge. This component should greet the player by name, but it was written with plain-HTML habits and React is unhappy.

Two things are wrong:
1. It uses the HTML attribute `class` — in JSX the attribute is `className`.
2. It prints the literal word `name` instead of rendering the `name` variable. In JSX you read a JavaScript value with curly braces: `{name}`.

Fix both so the page reads "Hello, Rank Challenger!" inside a styled card.
objectives: Render the variable with curly braces: `{name}`
hints: JSX is not HTML. `class` is a reserved word in JavaScript, so React uses `className`.
Anything inside `{ }` in JSX is evaluated as JavaScript. `{name}` prints the value of the variable.

checks.failMessage: Use `className` instead of the HTML `class` attribute.
Render the variable with curly braces: `{name}`.
